scope: Align MiroFish churn and retention checks with the same macro-aware, fit-aware signal family used during adoption advancement, then rerun the enterprise cluster playoff

decision_context.prior_state: The graph-fit tuning improved semantic domain priors, but the simulation still used raw awareness for churn and retention checks, creating a mismatch between what helped a persona adopt and what helped them persist.
decision_context.decision: Add shared effective-awareness helpers, use fit-adjusted awareness in churn and retention checks, rerun the symmetric enterprise cluster playoff, and save a new diagnostic brief plus methodology note.
decision_context.why_now: The enterprise diagnostics still showed large attention-to-retention and interest-to-choice gaps. Before changing thresholds again, the simulation should first stop judging persistence with weaker semantics than adoption.

files_expected.purpose: Adds shared awareness helpers and applies fit-aware, macro-aware signals to churn and retention checks.
Stores the enterprise cluster replay after retention-side signal symmetry.
Stores the diagnostic brief for the signal-symmetry replay.
Summarizes the symmetry fix and the resulting enterprise-cluster movement.
Captures the request, verification plan, and guardrails for this tuning tranche.

planned_steps: Apply fit-aware awareness to churn and retention checks
Rerun the symmetric enterprise cluster playoff
Build a diagnostic brief from the replay
Save the methodology note and update the transparent logs

expected_checks: The replay keeps questionnaire as the strongest enterprise ensemble domain
The replay materially improves RFP and compliance evidence relative to the graph-only tuned replay
The note records that maintained benchmark admission remains deferred

guardrails: Do not auto-admit any enterprise domain into maintained benchmarks
Keep the change inside the simulation layer and research artifacts
Use the replay to evaluate methodology quality, not to force a benchmark winner
